slides.title: Your muscle is a *blood-sugar sponge*.
slides.sub: Skeletal muscle is the biggest place your body parks glucose. More of it means better blood-sugar control — for energy, not just looks.

slides.title: Muscle stores your sugar
slides.body: Skeletal muscle is your largest *glucose sink* — it pulls sugar out of the blood and stores it.

slides.title: GLUT4 opens the door
slides.body: Training builds *GLUT4 transporters* — tiny doors that let muscle soak up glucose without needing much insulin.

slides.title: Better insulin sensitivity
slides.body: More muscle means *better insulin sensitivity* — steadier blood sugar, steadier energy across the whole day.

slides.title: Lift twice a week
slides.body: You don't need a bodybuilder physique — *two strength sessions a week* meaningfully improves how you handle carbs.

slides.title: Walk after meals
slides.body: A *10-minute walk* after eating tells your muscles to pull glucose from the blood right away.

slides.title: Build the sponge, *steady your energy*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your workouts, steps, and meals into one daily Health Score — so you can watch your metabolic habits add up.

caption:
Your muscle is basically a blood-sugar sponge. 💪

Skeletal muscle is the biggest place your body parks glucose — GLUT4 doors pull it out of the blood. Two strength sessions a week plus a short walk after meals can meaningfully improve how you handle carbs. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health scores your workouts, steps, and meals into one daily number → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why More Muscle Means Steadier Blood Sugar
